persona: B2B SaaS PM, 40 interviews in 5 days validating a pricing tier
prompt_to_designer: I need to figure out if we should charge $99/mo or $49/mo for our project management tool. Let's interview 40 mid-market PMs who currently use Jira or Asana.
expected_outline_topics: current pricing paid for PM tools

sample_answers.text: I'm a PM at a 200-person B2B startup, we use Jira today plus a mess of Notion pages.
Honestly $99 is stretching it, we cap tool budgets at $60/seat per month.
Jira feels heavy but it's what our engineers know. Linear has been eating our developer mindshare.
Our CTO approves anything over $50/seat. Under that I can expense it.
If it saved me 3 hours of standup prep per week I'd pay double.

expected_themes: Jira incumbent friction but high switching cost
budget owner is CTO not PM for anything > $50
time-saving on standup prep is a willingness-to-pay unlock
Linear is the competitive threat, not Asana
